title: prunus incisa f. urceolata
intro: Prunus incisa f. urceolata, also known as the Sawara or Japanese Sawara, is a type of flowering cherry tree native to Japan. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Prunus incisa f. urceolata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Prunus incisa f. urceolata can be used to make herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to soothe sore muscles and calm irritated skin.
